data.content:
I can see the project — one test expects `CalculateShipping("express", 1.0)` to return `15.00m`, but the method returns `10.00m`, so that test will fail.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` so tests re-run automatically when you save changes:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText: The user wants to use `dotnet watch test` for hot reload. Let me start it up in async mode so they can iterate on their code.

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is running. It now builds successfully and shows **1 passed, 1 failed**:

- ✅ `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5`
- ❌ `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — expects `15.00` but `CalculateShipping("express", ...)` returns `10.00`

The watcher is waiting for file changes — every time you save `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs`, it will automatically rebuild and re-run the tests. To fix the failing test, update the `"express"` case from `10.00m` to `15.00m` (or adjust the test expectation). Want me to fix it?
